posts_within_12h.title: Comparison of Axis foils. Which one is which?
posts_within_12h.text:
Comparison of Axis foils. Which one is which?

Answer options (pls answer in comment):
⬅️ Tempo 1090 | Fireball 1070 ➡️
⬅️ Tempo 1090 | Tempo 1090 ➡️
⬅️ Fireball 1070 | Tempo 1090 ➡️
⬅️ Fireball 1070 | Fireball 1070 ➡️

[Video comparison: two riders, one at 23 KM/H pace 2:40, one at 19 KM/H pace 3:08]

comments.text: Fun game! I would say: Fireball 1070 on the left, Tempo 1090 on the right. Same wingspan more or less, but Tempo is less draggy, which leads to less BPM overall.
yes but look the stability on the board .....

posts_within_12h.title: Mast Size Question
posts_within_12h.text: Does anyone prefer the 820mm over the 750mm for behind the boat or dock start? Pro?/cons? I'm looking for a power carbon mast and thinking 820mm to wakefoil, wingfoil, and dock start. I only want to buy one haha.

comments.text: I love my 820 for dock and wing, never use my old 750 anymore.
If you Wingfoil in rough seas or waves, the extra length is always useful for breach prevention, 82 is my sweet spot for wing, surf, dock, tow.
I hate anything over 80. Too long for me...
Got the 750 c and 90 Alu 19. I prefer the 90.
I bought the 750, and it's fine, but wish I had bought the 820mm

posts_11_24h.title: Surge/Spitfire recommendation for Gold Coast wave riding
posts_11_24h.text: I am looking at buying a surge (or spitfire) on the Gold Coast Australia. I am 85kg, intermediate fliteboard L2 efoil rider (Axis wing adaptor) just starting to ride waves (rode 18 little rides last session). Any recommendations on size? Not sure how small I would enjoy and don't have funds for multiple wings. Thanks Paul

comments.text: I have a 1100 spit fire glides great on small waves I'm moving to armstrong so make me a stupid offer

I'm 85kg live on the Gold Coast as well. I have most of the Surges. If it was just one - for me I'd go the 830 - ride mostly at the Alley, winging in waves. Check in with SurfFx - they have demos... take out the guesswork

Spitfire is a great wing as well. I've had all of them. 780 was my daily driver for the Alley before I got the Surges. DM me and if I'm down at the Alley, you could give one of mine a try if you can't demo one from SurfFx.

posts_11_24h.title: Front foil recommendation - 65kg parawing/surf/dockstart
posts_11_24h.text: Hi, I'am looking for advice for a new front. I'am 65kg, For parawing, surfoiling <1m, dockstart full speed. Surge looks good but Which size 890? 950 or 830? Or fireball? I am afraid about thin chord and high aspect. Thank for your help

posts_11_24h.title: HPS 930 vs ART V2 879 comparison - wingfoil 91kg
posts_11_24h.text: Shout out to my old HPS 930 with the 380 speed stab. 5.0 wing, 92L board, 91kg rider, 11-17kn thermal side off wind, 8°C water. Amazing experience with HPS 930. While I have been using my most recent purchase, the ART V2 879 the HPS feels like I am on a full...

official_page_scan.most_recent_post_topic: Surge desert colorway - product design inspiration
official_page_scan.notes: No new posts on official page in last 24h. Most recent post was Feb 8 (Surge colorway), Jan 18 (Boot Düsseldorf). 6.1K followers.

product_comparisons: Tempo 1090 vs Fireball 1070 - video comparison showing speed/BPM differences. Community consensus: Fireball 1070 = left (more BPM), Tempo 1090 = right (less draggy, less BPM)
820mm vs 750mm mast - strong community preference for 820mm for versatility (dock, wing, wake, surf). Multiple experienced riders say 820 is their go-to.
product_recommendations: 85kg Gold Coast rider: Community recommends Surge 830 for wave riding (Sean Richardson, Top contributor)
65kg multi-discipline: Steven Fisher recommends Fireball for parawing/dock, Surge for prone surf
dealer_mentions: SurfFx (Gold Coast) mentioned as offering demo sessions for Surge lineup
